subject: Sociology
title: New Religious Movements
description: A short lesson to help you apply New Religious Movements.

hook.text: People call them cults. Sociologists call them new religious movements. The difference matters.

buildup.text: NRMs are small, recent groups that often form around a charismatic leader with fresh spiritual teachings.

discovery.text: Most NRMs are harmless. They attract seekers dissatisfied with mainstream religion or secular emptiness.

twist.text: The label cult is a power move. Established religions were all new once and faced the same accusations.

climax.text: Studying NRMs reveals how all religions begin: with outsiders offering answers the mainstream can't.

punchline.text: Every religion started as the new one.

quiz.question: Why do sociologists prefer 'new religious movement' over 'cult'?
options: It's a more neutral, analytical term
